rulings.Case Title: JIMMY WONG VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff filed a statement of non-opposition in which he concedes the motion to compel and agrees to an order compelling service of plaintiff's responses to written discovery no later than December 5, 2019 and awarding sanctions not to exceed $860 against attorney James Braden only. Accordingly, the motion is granted as follows: Plaintiff shall serve written responses on or before December 5, 2019 to the following written discovery propounded by defendant San Francisco Housing Authority ("SFHA"): Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One. Plaintiff shall further produce all documents required to be produced by plaintiff's responses on or before December 5, 2019. Plaintiff's attorney James Braden shall pay to SFHA on or before December 5, 2019 the sum of $860 representing sanctions for discovery abuse. The motion is otherwise denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions And For A Protective Order Against Anna Gatti For Failure To Attend And Proceed With Noticed Deposition Under Ccp 2025.430, 2025.420; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 2, (Part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC.'s Motion For Sanctions And For A Protective Order Against Anna Gatti For Failure To Attend And Proceed With Noticed Deposition Under CCP 2025.430, 2025.420 Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Loop AI Labs Inc. ("Loop") and Gianmauro Calafiore ("Calafiore") seek sanctions and a protective order against plaintiff Anna Gatti for failure to appear at the deposition of Calafiore which plaintiff had noticed for San Francisco on October 15, 2019. Said notice of deposition was preceded by two similar notices, both of which were objected to on multiple grounds. The parties had numerous communications concerning the deposition of Calafiore but no agreement was ever reached concerning it. It is uncontested that Calafiore is a resident of Milan, Italy. Accordingly, each notice of his deposition for San Francisco was beyond the 75 mile radius specified by Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.250 and no motion under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.260 for a deposition at a more distant location was made. After service of the notice of deposition for October 15, 2019, the parties exchanged objections and communications concerning the deposition without reaching any agreement. The last communication was by plaintiff on September 26, 2019 (Ex. 6 to Decl. of Healy, Ex. 16 to Decl. of Gatti) wherein she "suggest[ed] that you make that date of the third notice of deposition work for the economy of this trial." Neither Calafiore or defendants' counsel ever announced a refusal to appear on October 15, 2019. On October 15, 2019, Calafiore and two attorneys for Loop and Calafiore appeared for Calafiore's deposition at the appointed location. Plaintiff failed to appear without excuse or substantial justification. (tentative ruling continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Loop Ai Labs Inc.'S Motion For Sanctions And For A Protective Order Against Anna Gatti For Failure To Attend And Proceed With Noticed Deposition Under Ccp 2025.430, 2025.420 (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 2, (Part 2 of 2 for tentative ruling) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry.) DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC.'s Motion For Sanctions And For A Protective Order Against Anna Gatti For Failure To Attend And Proceed With Noticed Deposition Under CCP 2025.430, 2025.420 Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.430 requires the imposition of sanctions where the party giving notice fails to appear for the deposition without substantial justification or other circumstances making the imposition of sanctions unjust. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate substantial justification for her failure to appear for the deposition on October 15, 2019 or other circumstances making the imposition of sanctions unjust. none of which are present here. Defendants' reasonable attorneys fees and expenses incurred in preparing for and appearing at the deposition scheduled for October 15, 2019 and in making this motion are the sum of $10,711.25. .According, plaintiff Gatti is ordered to pay sanctions to defendants Loop and Calafiore in the amount $10,711.25 within ten days of the date of the order hereon. The motion is otherwise denied. In particular, the request for a protective order is denied as premature and as not accompanied with a meet and confer declaration as required by Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.420. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC WINE DISTRIBUTORS, INC.'s Motion For Leave To Conduct Discovery Of Defendants Financial Condition Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for financial discovery is denied for lack of adequate notice pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 1005 and lack of an adequate showing pursuant to Civil Code Section 3295(c). Among other deficiencies, the moving papers fail establish any grounds on which punitive damages could be awarded and the declaration of Burnet fails to establish percipient knowledge supporting the conclusions asserted therein. rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LUJAN VS. DAVID SMALL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT EDUARDO RALLO DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Eduardo Rollo's demurrer to the third amended complaint (TAC) is sustained without leave to amend on statute of limitations grounds. Kimomex Corporation operated a Mexican grocery from 2007 to 2010. Plaintiff Albert Lujan was Kimomex's CEO, CFO and secretary. (TAC 7:11-15.) Lujan was also Kimomex's responsible managing employee (RME), making him personally liable for its unpaid sales, employment and income taxes. (Id.) Lujan pleads that "commencing in 2014, taxing authorities have seized on" him "to satisfy these liabilities based on his status as RME." (Id. at 9:12-15.) On August 23, 2019, Lujan filed his TAC; it pleads a single cause of action, for "indemnity and contribution." It is undisputed that the statute of limitations for these claims is two years. (Am. States Ins. Co. v. National Fire Ins. Co. of Hartford (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 692, 698-99.) Lujan argues that "continuing injury" extends the statutory period, because he is paying down Kimomex's past taxes over time. However, the injury was sustained when the tax obligations accrued and Lujan became liable for them, not as Lujan pays them off years later. The statute of limitations has long since run. The cases Lujan relies on are not to the contrary. In Howard Jarvis Taxpayers Assn. v. City of La Habra (1001) 25 Cal.4th 809, 819, a tax was imposed into the future, not just assessed in the past. In Aryeh v. Canon Business Solutions, Inc. (2013) 55 Cal.4th 1185, defendant continued to make excess charges during a limitations period. Given the above rulings, Rallo's other demurrer arguments need not be addressed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 25, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANTs ANDREW G. PRICE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND D/B/A ARTISANAL, ARTISANAL TALENT GROUP, INC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One; (2) Plaintiffs Amended Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set One (3) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; (4) Plaintiffs Amended Response To Defendants Form Interrogatories-General, Set Two; And (5) For Appointment Of Discovery Referee Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel further responses is granted to the following extent: Requests for Production: No. 12 is granted as to separate specification of grounds for each redaction. Form Interrogatories Set 1: No. 2.6 is granted as to last known address of "Bluespan"; No. 8.7 is granted as to calculation of elements of claimed damage; No. 50.1 is granted as to identification of "other emails" referred to; No. 50.2 is granted as to the amount of money or number of shares claimed to be owed and identification of the defendant referred to. Form Interrogatories Set 2: No. 17.1, Request for Admission No. 1, is granted as to identification of documents. Form Interrogatories Employment Law: No. 200.3 is granted as to identification of documents; No. 210.2 is granted as to calculation of the elements of damages claimed; No. 210.3 is granted as to identification of the derogatory statements, employers and venture capitalists referred to; No. 212.4 is granted as to the last known address and telephone number of Dr. Heilman; No. 212.7 is granted as to identification of the "recommended specialist". The motion is otherwise denied.
